content.full_text:
October 20, 1989


Jim Johnson
Supervisor, District Four
County of San Luis Obispo
County Government Center
San Luis Obispo, CA  93408

Re: Letter No. 89-595

Dear Mr. Johnson:

You have requested confirmation of advice under the Political
Reform Act. We have reviewed your request and determined that it
is not appropriate for response with a simple confirmation.
Therefore, we will be preparing a fuller analysis of the issues
raised in your letter to us. After the analysis has been prepared
and approved, we will forward it to you as quickly as we can.

If you have any questions, you may contact me at (916)
322-5901.

Sincerely,

Blanca M. Breeze
Counsel, Legal Division

BMB:plh:confadv2


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

BOARD OF SUPERVISORS

COUNTY GOVERNMENT CENTER • SAN LUIS OBISPO, CALIFORNIA 93408 • 805-549-5450

October 13, 1989

Kathryn E. Donovan, General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95814

Subject:  Conflict of Interest
          Supervisor James Johnson
          San Luis Obispo County

Dear Ms. Donovan,

     I have been persistently questioned about my participation
in the October 6th hearing on an interim growth ordinance.

     In a telephone conversation on October 5 with Blanka Breeze
of your agency, I asked for and received permission to record
the conversation.

     I relied on the telephone advise given by Blanka Breeze,
that I could participate in the hearing as long as there was no
discussion of subdivisions.

     I am asking that all correspondence relating to this case be
held until such time as written confirmation of the telephone
conversation can be included.

Sincerely,

Jim Johnson
Jim Johnson

JAMES JOHNSON
DISTRICT FOUR

FPPC TRANSMITTAL OF CORRESPONDENCE

CORRESPONDENT: Johnson, Jim                    ADVICE #: 89 259 [circled]
              San Luis Obispo, CA.

RE: Confirm phone advice - participation in hrg.

DATE REC'D: 10/18/89              DATE DUE: 11/16/89

conf. adv. 2
LETTER SENT:_____________ CC TO:_________________________

REQUEST FOR ADD'L INFO SENT:_____________________________

COMMENTS: Please let him know ASAP that
we cannot confirm this advice


KATHY           JOHN W.         MARGARET
LILLY           JILL            CHERYL
JOHN Mc         JEEVAN          BLANCA
JEANETTE        SCOTT           TARA
JON R.          JOE


In a telephone conversation on October 5 with Blanka Breeze
of your agency, I asked for and received permission to record
the conversation.

I relied on the telephone advise given by Blanka Breeze,
that I could participate in the hearing as long as there was no
discussion of subdivisions.

I am asking that all correspondence relating to this case be
held until such time as written confirmation of the telephone
conversation can be included.

Sincerely,

[signature]

embedding.qa_text: This is a letter from the California Fair Political Practices Commission dated October 20, 1989, addressed to Jim Johnson, Supervisor of District Four in San Luis Obispo County. The letter responds to Mr. Johnson's request for confirmation of advice under the Political Reform Act. The commission determined that a simple confirmation was not appropriate and will instead prepare a fuller analysis of the issues raised in Mr. Johnson's letter. The commission states it will forward this analysis to Mr. Johnson as quickly as possible. If Mr. Johnson has any questions, he may contact the sender at (916) 322-5901. The letter is signed by Blanca M. Breeze, Counsel, Legal Division, and includes a reference code: BMB:plh:confadv2. October 13, 1989 Kathryn E. Donovan, General Counsel Fair Political Practices Commission Post Office Box 807 Sacramento, CA 95814 Subject: Conflict of Interest Supervisor James Johnson San Luis Obispo County Dear Ms. Donovan, I have been persistently questioned about my participation in the October 6th hearing on an interim growth ordinance. In a telephone conversation on October 5 with Blanka Breeze of your agency, I asked for and received permission to record the conversation. I relied on the telephone advise given by Blanka Breeze, that I could participate in the hearing as long as there was no discussion of subdivisions. I am asking that all correspondence relating to this case be held until such time as written confirmation of the telephone conversation can be included. Sincerely, Jim Johnson FPPC TRANSMITTAL OF CORRESPONDENCE CORRESPONDENT: Johnson, Jim San Luis Obispo, CA. RE: Confirm phone advice - participation in hrg.
